results.document:
[chunk 2]
Title: [2512.03324] Cache What Lasts: Token Retention for Memory-Bounded KV Cache in LLMs
URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.03324
Section: 

-memory regimes. Remarkably, it even surpasses full-cache models in some settings, showing that selective retention can serve as a form of regularization, suppressing noise from uninformative tokens. Qualitative analyses further reveal that learned retention scores align with human intuition, naturally recovering heuristics such as sink tokens, sliding windows, and gist compression without explicit design. Beyond efficiency, retention scores provide insights into layer- and head-specific roles, suggesting a new path toward LLM interpretability.

Subjects:

Machine Learning (cs.LG)

; Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)

Cite as:

arXiv:2512.03324

[cs.LG]

(or

arXiv:2512.03324v2

[cs.LG]

for this version)
